title: sambucus racemosa f. purpurea
intro: Sambucus racemosa f. purpurea, also known as the purple elderberry, is a variety of the Sambucus racemosa plant, which is a shrub or small tree native to North America. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiviral and antimicrobial compounds, which are found in its berries and flowers. The purple elderberry is said to have various health benefits, including reducing the severity and duration of colds and flu, as well as acting as an anti-inflammatory agent to soothe digestive issues and skin irritations. Herbal preparations of the purple elderberry can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and supplements, often used to boost immune function and support overall well-being.
